summary: Gallai's 1968 conjecture — that every connected graph on $n$ vertices decomposes into at most $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ paths — remains open in general. Since the OPG posting date (2013) it has been verified for large families: graphs of maximum degree at most 5 (Bonamy–Perrett, 2016), graphs whose E-subgraph lies in a specific block family (Botler–Sambinelli, 2019), connected planar graphs except $K_3$ and $K_5^-$ (Blanché–Bonamy–Bonichon, 2021), and 2-degenerate graphs (Anto–Basavaraju, 2022). The full conjecture for all connected graphs is still unresolved.

since_posted.title: Gallai's path decomposition conjecture for graphs of small maximum degree
since_posted.claim: Verifies Gallai's conjecture for every connected graph of maximum degree at most 5.

since_posted.title: Towards Gallai's path decomposition conjecture
since_posted.venue: arXiv preprint (later in J. Graph Theory)
since_posted.claim: Verifies the conjecture for the family of graphs whose E-subgraph is a subgraph of a specific block family $\mathcal{G}$, generalising Fan's 2005 result.

since_posted.title: Gallai's path decomposition in planar graphs
since_posted.authors: Alexandre Blanché, Marthe Bonamy, Nicolas Bonichon
since_posted.claim: Proves that every connected planar graph except $K_3$ and $K_5^-$ decomposes into at most $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$ paths.

since_posted.title: Gallai's Path Decomposition for 2-degenerate Graphs
since_posted.claim: Shows that any connected 2-degenerate graph on $n$ vertices, other than the triangle, decomposes into at most $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$ paths.

search_queries: Gallai conjecture path decomposition (n+1)/2 connected graph proof
arxiv Gallai conjecture paths connected graph decomposition
notes: The conjecture is also reported verified for treewidth ≤ 3 (Botler et al., 2020, J. Graph Theory) and treewidth ≤ 4 (more recent work), and for block graphs (AIMS Math, 2025); these were not verified in detail here.
